data.title: Issues in managing open innovation-based supplier networks in Industry 5.0 context
data.authors: Muhammad Faraz Mubarak, Richard Evans, Monika Petraite, Giedrius Jucevicius, Khuram Shahzad
data.description: This study explores the critical challenges associated with managing open innovation-driven supply chain networks within the context of Industry 5.0. By utilizing a two-phase methodology, the research first identifies 14 challenges in 5 different categories through open-ended interviews with 27 experts from European industry and academia. These challenges are then prioritized using the analytical hierarchical process (AHP) involving 21 experts who ranked the challenges based on their perceived importance. The findings highlight that clearly defined roles and responsibilities, effective change management, intellectual property (IP) management, and trust-building are critical for the successful implementation of open innovation strategies. Additionally, challenges such as managing the heterogeneous sizes and strategic visions of partners, addressing technological readiness and adoption issues, and safeguarding …
